root_text.an4.108:1.8: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, cattāro balībaddūpamā puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.108:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo sagavacaṇḍo hoti, no paragavacaṇḍo?
root_text.an4.108:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo sakaparisaṁ ubbejetā hoti, no paraparisaṁ.
root_text.an4.108:2.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo sagavacaṇḍo hoti, no paragavacaṇḍo.
root_text.an4.108:2.4: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, balībaddo sagavacaṇḍo, no paragavacaṇḍo;
root_text.an4.108:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo paragavacaṇḍo hoti, no sagavacaṇḍo?
root_text.an4.108:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo paraparisaṁ ubbejetā hoti, no sakaparisaṁ.
root_text.an4.108:3.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo paragavacaṇḍo hoti, no sagavacaṇḍo.
root_text.an4.108:3.4: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, balībaddo paragavacaṇḍo, no sagavacaṇḍo;
root_text.an4.108:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo sagavacaṇḍo ca hoti paragavacaṇḍo ca?
root_text.an4.108:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo sakaparisaṁ ubbejetā hoti paraparisañca.
root_text.an4.108:4.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo sagavacaṇḍo ca hoti paragavacaṇḍo ca.
root_text.an4.108:4.4: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, balībaddo sagavacaṇḍo ca paragavacaṇḍo ca;
root_text.an4.108:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo neva sagavacaṇḍo hoti no paragavacaṇḍo?
root_text.an4.108:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo neva sakaparisaṁ ubbejetā hoti, no paraparisañca.
root_text.an4.108:5.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo neva sagavacaṇḍo hoti, no paragavacaṇḍo.
root_text.an4.108:5.4: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, balībaddo neva sagavacaṇḍo, no paragavacaṇḍo;
root_text.an4.108:5.6: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro balībaddūpamā puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.108:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four kinds of oxen.
translation_text.an4.108:1.3: One hostile to its own herd, not others;
translation_text.an4.108:1.4: one hostile to other herds, not its own;
translation_text.an4.108:1.5: one hostile to both its own herd and others; and
translation_text.an4.108:1.6: one hostile to neither its own herd nor others.
translation_text.an4.108:1.7: These are the four kinds of oxen.
translation_text.an4.108:1.8: In the same way, these four individuals similar to oxen are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.108:1.10: One hostile to their own herd, not others;
translation_text.an4.108:1.11: one hostile to other herds, not their own;
translation_text.an4.108:1.12: one hostile to both their own herd and others; and
translation_text.an4.108:1.13: one hostile to neither their own herd nor others.
translation_text.an4.108:2.1: And how is an individual hostile to their own herd, not others?
translation_text.an4.108:2.2: It’s when an individual intimidates their own followers, not the followers of others.
translation_text.an4.108:2.3: That’s how an individual is hostile to their own herd, not others.
translation_text.an4.108:2.4: That individual is like an ox that’s hostile to its own herd, not others.
translation_text.an4.108:3.1: And how is an individual hostile to other herds, not their own?
translation_text.an4.108:3.2: It’s when an individual intimidates the followers of others, not their own. …
translation_text.an4.108:4.1: And how is an individual hostile to both their own herd and others?
translation_text.an4.108:4.2: It’s when an individual intimidates their own followers and the followers of others. …
translation_text.an4.108:5.1: And how is an individual hostile to neither their own herd nor others?
translation_text.an4.108:5.2: It’s when an individual doesn’t intimidate their own followers or the followers of others.
translation_text.an4.108:5.6: These four individuals similar to oxen are found in the world.”

variant_text.an4.108:1.1: balībaddā → balivaddā (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed); balibaddhā (mr)
